description_long: This is a small, cylindrical glass jar with a wide, metallic screw-on lid that appears weathered and slightly tarnished. The jar is wrapped with an aged, yellowed paper label featuring faded red and green text, some of which is partially illegible due to wear and creasing. The label includes both printed and possibly stamped elements, with a circular red emblem visible on one side. The glass body of the jar is dark, possibly tinted, and the contents are not visible, suggesting it may be opaque or filled with a dark substance. The overall appearance is vintage, with signs of age and handling, such as discoloration and minor abrasions on both the label and the lid. This object would likely be found in an old pharmacy, medical supply store, or as a discarded item in an urban environment.
description: A vintage glass medicine jar with a tarnished metal lid and a worn, yellowed paper label featuring faded red and green text. The jar has a dark, opaque body and shows signs of age and use.
description_view_0: The front view shows the jar's metallic lid and the most legible section of the label, with large red text and some green writing visible.
description_view_1: This view highlights the right side of the label, where more green text is visible, including a name and location, with the lid's texture clearly shown.
description_view_2: The back view reveals the dark, opaque glass with the label's edge visible, showing less text and more of the jar's body.
description_view_3: This view displays the left side of the label, featuring a red circular emblem and more faded text, with the lid and label's wear evident.
attribute_label_text: "MROZOL" Mokotowska Fabryka Chem.-Farm. A. Gąsecki i S-wie w Warszawie [illegible] Nereg 28 [illegible]
